data.title: e.l.f. Beauty lists 2.5M merger-issued shares for sale by holders
data.description:
• E.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) filed a shelf registration with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission pertaining to the sale of ~2.5M shares by stockholders related to a merger agreement of HRBeauty, the parent company of rhode.
  • E.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) is not selling any shares under this prospectus and will not receive any proceeds from the sale by the selling stockholders.
  • Under the terms of the merger agreement, e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) agreed to acquire rhode for $800 million at closing, comprised of $600 million of cash and $200 million, or approximately 2.5 million shares, of newly issued shares of e.l.f. Beauty common stock issued to existing equity holders of rhode,
  • In accordance with the company’s obligations under the merger agreement, e.l.f. (ELF) agreed to register the resale of these shares offered by the selling shareholders.

data.title: Ocugen launches $20 million direct offering of common stock, warrants
data.description:
• Ocugen (NASDAQ:OCGN) has entered into a securities purchase agreement with Janus Henderson Investors to purchase 20M shares of common stock and warrants for gross proceeds of $20 million
  • The company may receive up to $30 million of additional gross proceeds if the warrants are exercised in full.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about August 11, 2025.
  • OCGN -1.83% after hours to $0.9801.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ATN International reaffirms 2025 guidance and raises dividend 15% amid ongoing fiber expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ATN International, Inc. (ATNI) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Brad W. Martin, CEO, began by emphasizing continued progress on long-term strategic priorities and noted that "our Q2 results were in line with our expectations and reflect the steps we're taking to optimize our cost structure." He highlighted a 1% revenue decline year-over-year, a 6% decrease in adjusted EBITDA, and a 2% rise in net cash from operations to approximately $60 million.
  • Martin announced, "our Board of Directors approved a 15% increase in our quarterly dividend, raising it up to $0.275 per share. This reflects the confidence we have in the underlying strength and resilience of our cash flow."
  • Martin reported an 8% increase in broadband homes passed by high-speed data services and a 1% growth in the high-speed subscriber base year-over-year. He stated the company is "capitalizing on our fiber, fiber-fed and broader network infrastructure to deliver essential high-value services in underserved markets."
  • In the International segment, Martin noted "postpaid subscribers grew by 4% year-over-year," and customers purchasing and consuming data plans rose by 25% year-over-year. Mobile churn rates improved by more than 5% for the second quarter in a row, and high-speed data ARPU and subscriber churn both improved by 3% year-over-year.
  • For the U.S. segment, Martin indicated "consumer broadband subscriber base grew more than 10% this quarter, and we are seeing positive trends in ARPU." He also highlighted ongoing investments in over $300 million in broadband infrastructure initiatives backed by government funding, with more than half slated for completion in 2025.
  • Carlos R. Doglioli, CFO, stated, "Total company revenue for the quarter was $181.3 million, down 1% year-over-year... Operating income for the second quarter decreased to $0.2 million versus $24.3 million in the year ago period."

Outlook

  • Martin reaffirmed the company's full year guidance as outlined during the prior quarter's call, stating, "we are reaffirming our full year guidance as outlined during our fourth quarter earnings call."
  • Doglioli specified, "We continue to expect revenue for the year to be in line with 2024 of $725 million, excluding construction revenue, adjusted EBITDA to be essentially flat with last year's $184 million. Net capital expenditures between $90 million and $100 million, down from $110.3 million in 2024 and net debt ratios to remain flat to year-end 2024 with a slight potential improvement exiting the year."
  • Management expects the second half to contribute a larger share of full year results, and residual restructuring and reorganization expenses in Q3, but at a lower level than Q2.

Financial Results

  • Doglioli reported, "Total company revenue for the quarter was $181.3 million, down 1% year-over-year." Operating income was $0.2 million, which compares to $24.3 million in the prior year, a period that included a $15.9 million gain from asset disposition.
  • Net loss for the quarter was $7 million or $0.56 per share, compared to net income of $9 million or $0.50 per share in the prior year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $45.8 million, down 6% from the prior year, mainly due to the impact of lower U.S. Telecom revenues.
  • The company ended the quarter with $113.3 million in cash and total debt of $583.4 million. Net cash provided by operating activities for the first half of the year increased 2% to approximately $60 million.
  • Capital expenditures for the first half of 2025 totaled $42 million, net of $45.9 million in reimbursable capital spend.

Q&A

  • Richard Hamilton Prentiss, Raymond James: Asked about the impact of the BBB bill. Doglioli replied, "it hasn't had an impact. Given our jurisdictions and tax positions, we're not expecting an impact in the short term."
  • Prentiss inquired about competition and access to labor/materials in fiber deployment. Martin responded, "those policies to help -- really help expedite permitting are going to be very helpful for us... So we do expect that's going to help speed up pace." On labor, Martin added, "we're seeing people coming to us quite regularly looking for work. So, no, we've not seen that yet."
  • Prentiss asked about the potential for converting the fiber business into a REIT. Martin answered, "that's not something we've looked at... there are other mechanisms out there, asset-backed securitization... But from a REIT perspective, not yet."
  • Gregory John Burns, Sidoti: Asked about inflection in U.S. fiber-led service growth. Martin replied, "we are optimistic with the progress we've made with our fiber builds, and we are seeing demand from the carriers starting to increase... we are expecting to continue to see improvements here in the second half of the year."
  • Burns also asked about competitive dynamics internationally. Martin noted, "the initial new entrant in our largest market, that was disruptive last year... has normalized to a degree... the competitive pressures are still there."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral, focused on understanding impacts of legislation, competition, and growth inflection points, with no overt skepticism or negative sentiment.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone in both prepared remarks and responses, emphasizing execution, cost control, and optimism for the second half. Martin stated, "We are focused on disciplined execution grounded in financial responsibility and confident in the strategic path we've chosen."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment remained steady with a focus on operational progress and future growth, though management showed slightly more confidence with the dividend increase and progress on strategic projects.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, with the outlook reaffirmed and expectations for the second half underscored in both quarters.
  • Strategic focus continues on cost control, operational efficiency, and fiber expansion, with a notable increase in dividend and more detailed updates on infrastructure initiatives this quarter.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on subsidy program impacts, monetization of investments, and competitive dynamics.
  • Key metrics such as revenue and adjusted EBITDA were similar quarter-over-quarter, though Q2 saw increased restructuring charges and marginal growth in cash flow.
  • Management's confidence level remained stable, with slightly more positive forward-looking commentary this quarter, especially regarding U.S. fiber build progress and dividend actions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the wind down of subsidy programs and decommissioning of legacy services as key headwinds impacting revenue.
  • Ongoing restructuring and reorganization charges were noted, with expectations for lower charges in Q3.
  • Competitive pressures, especially in international prepaid mobile markets, remain an area of focus.
  • Management is monitoring policy changes, trade, and tariff developments, stating they "remain confident in our ability to absorb any short-term effects within the parameters of our current 2025 financial guidance."

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that ATN International is executing with discipline, reaffirming its 2025 financial guidance while raising the quarterly dividend by 15% as a sign of confidence in its cash flow and strategic direction. The company continues to prioritize operational efficiency, network expansion—particularly in fiber—and stabilization of its U.S. and International segments, positioning itself for improved performance in the second half of the year and sustainable long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Soluna announces CFO transition
data.description:
• Soluna (NASDAQ:SLNH) announced on Friday that it has accepted the resignation of John Tunison, chief financial officer and treasurer, effective August 21st.
  • The company said Tunison is departing from his role at Soluna to accept a similar position with a privately held company.
  • David Michaels, a longtime board member filling various roles at the company over his tenure, including chairman of the board, lead independent director, and chairman of the audit committee, has assumed the role of Interim CFO and Treasurer. Michaels previously served as interim CFO of the company in 2023
  • SLNH +1.49% after hours to $0.4699.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Petrobras plunges after pouring cold water on extra dividend hopes
data.description:
Petrobras (NYSE:PBR) closed -7.3% on Friday after posting weaker than expected Q2 results and warning it sees little chance of paying extraordinary dividends this year.

Q2 swung to a net profit of 26.65B reais (~$4.9B) from a net loss of 2.61B reais in the year-earlier quarter, when results were affected by a major tax dispute, but net revenues fell 2.6% Y/Y to 119.13B reais, as the company said Brent oil prices averaged $67.82/bbl in the quarter, compared to $75.66/bbl in Q1.

Q2 total hydrocarbon production jumped 5% Q/Q to 2.91M bbl/day, and the company maintained full-year guidance of 2.8M boe/day with 4% leeway.

Petrobras (NYSE:PBR) announced dividends and interest on equity to shareholders of 8.66B reais (~$1.6B), but CFO Fernando Melgarejo said on its earnings conference call.

"Although we would very much like to have the excess cash to make an extraordinary payment, we see a low probability this year," the CFO said, citing lower revenues because of weaker global oil prices.

Petrobras' (PBR) Q2 capital spending totaled $4.41B, mainly at large projects in the Santos Basin, and Melgarejo reaffirmed the company's $18.5B capital spending outlook for the full year, but added that some projects could be revised to take the lower oil prices into account.

data.title: Skechers tops sales estimates as EMEA outperforms, China lags
data.description:
Skechers U.S.A. (NYSE:SKX) reported mixed results for the second quarter as the footwear manufacturer saw solid gains in both direct-to-consumer and wholesale channels, driving up the company’s sales by more than 13%.

The company earned an adjusted profit of $0.83 per share, down 8% from a year ago and 3 cents below expectations. On an unadjusted basis, the company reported a profit of $1.13 per share versus. Gross margin was compressed by 160 basis points to 53.3%.

By channel, wholesale sales were up 15% to $1.30B, and DTC sales were up 11% to $1.14B, driving total sales to $2.44B, beating the consensus estimate of $2.35B.

Regionally, sales were strongest in Europe, Middle East & Africa (EMEA) with a 48.5% increase, followed by a 5.5% gain in Asia Pacific (APAC), and 1.1% in the Americas. Sales in China continued to lag, falling by another 8.2% after a 16% decline in the previous quarter.

The company did not provide guidance for Q3 or FY25.
